themes.exampleQuote: Delicious soup dumplings, great mixed variety. Great first experience.
Our server Morgan had an amazing smile and great customer service.
We tried the new spicy beef dumplings and sweet and sour soup — both were incredible.
This is going to be a repeat when we come back to town for sure.
Everything was great, but the bathroom was not clean.
Didi the robot was fun to watch.

notable_quote: Our server Morgan had an amazing smile and great customer service — she held amazing conversation and made the experience very welcoming and rememberable.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers arrive for the soup dumplings and leave talking about their server by name — Morgan, Galilea, Chavely appear across multiple reviews — while the one persistent friction point is bathroom upkeep in an otherwise clean, quick-service room.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-nine Pittsburgh creators filmed Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings this quarter without any prompting from the restaurant itself — 615,199 TikTok plays generated entirely from the outside looking in, the business capturing none of that audience into its own feed. @lilianwesthafer alone pulled 116,900 plays on a caption that barely mentioned the food. Nan Xiang holds the top spot across all Pittsburgh restaurants and all Pittsburgh businesses in this issue, its signal anchored by a flawless conversion path and a visual catalog — 1,349 photos across 15 Google categories — that signals a restaurant the city has decided to document with or without permission. The rank stays here as long as the TikTok creator wave keeps running; what would move it further is the restaurant finally showing up in its own story.
tldr_read: Pittsburgh's most-filmed restaurant this quarter, with a watertight Google presence and 615K TikTok plays it didn't post itself — Instagram engagement is the one axis still underperforming. Icons of the Burgh, #1 in Pittsburgh Restaurants and #1 in Pittsburgh Businesses.
tldr_meaning: The signals that put Nan Xiang at the top — creator volume, review depth, conversion completeness — are externally driven and holding strong; the only data gap that could eventually cost rank position is the low 0.21% Instagram engagement rate on an account that is posting consistently but not yet pulling its weight.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Six Hundred Fifteen Thousand Plays, Zero Of Them Posted By The Restaurant Itself

playbook.headline: Turn TikTok audience into Instagram followers
playbook.action: With 615K TikTok plays generated by 29 outside creators, cross-posting even one top-performing creator clip per week to Instagram Reels would directly address the 0.21% engagement gap.

playbook.headline: Fix the one consistent service complaint
playbook.action: Bathroom cleanliness surfaces in reviews as the only recurring negative against an otherwise clean, fast-service reputation — a visible ops checklist during peak hours closes that loop before it compounds.

playbook.headline: Claim a TikTok presence before it drifts
playbook.action: Twenty-nine creators have already built the visual language for this restaurant — a business TikTok account that reposts, responds, or duets with that existing footage converts a passive audience into a owned signal.
